tools.name: trouver_expert
tools.description: Call this when the user needs a trustworthy real-estate professional in a Canadian city or neighbourhood: real-estate broker, mortgage broker, home inspector, notary/real-estate lawyer, or appraiser. Returns the Payotte-verified expert(s): name, score /100, licence number + official registry link so

tools.name: verifier_titre
tools.description: Call this when the user wants to know whether a real-estate profession is regulated in a Canadian province, which body regulates it, and where to verify a licence or membership. Returns the regulator, the public registry URL when one exists, and whether the credential is a mandatory licence, a profe

tools.name: stats_marche
tools.description: Call this for current housing-market figures in a Canadian city: reference price (MLS HPI benchmark or median), year-over-year change, sales volume, months of inventory, days on market, 5-year growth. Compiled by Payotte from real-estate board and CREA publications; each city lists its sources.

tools.name: contacter_expert
tools.description: Call this ONLY when the user explicitly asks to contact, reach out to, or request a quote/appointment from a Payotte-verified professional. Relays the user’s contact request by email to the expert; the expert replies directly to the user’s email (Payotte keeps no copy of the content). BEFORE calling

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "contacter_expert" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Call this ONLY when the user explicitly asks to contact, reach out to, or request a quote/appointment from a Payotte-verified professional. Relays the user’s co…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: contacter_expert · Private data: trouver_expert, verifier_titre, contacter_expert · Public sink: contacter_expert.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: contacter_expert · Private data: trouver_expert, verifier_titre, contacter_expert · Public sink: contacter_expert

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
